data.body:
Goal:
Make stale beat grooming own its agent execution timeout and failure reporting instead of reusing scope-refinement-specific timeout labels. Stale grooming should fail loudly with stale-grooming-specific logs, review errors, and user-visible status when its agent subprocess hangs, and the timeout should be configurable or intentionally documented separately from scope refinement.

Verification:
1. Run the stale grooming unit tests with `bun run test -- src/lib/__tests__/stale-beat-grooming*.test.ts` and confirm a simulated hung agent fails with a stale-grooming-specific timeout message.
2. Run `bun run test -- src/lib/__tests__/scope-refinement*.test.ts` and confirm scope refinement still enforces its existing timeout behavior.
3. Trigger or mock `POST /api/beats/stale-grooming/reviews` for a stale beat with a hanging agent and confirm `GET /api/beats/stale-grooming/status` records the failed review with a message that names stale grooming, not scope refinement.
4. Inspect server logs for the timeout path and confirm emitted log tags use `[stale-grooming]` or another stale-grooming-specific marker rather than only `[scope-refinement]`.

Constraints:
Do not change the Knots workflow graph or terminal-state behavior. Keep the fix scoped to stale grooming/scope-refinement prompt execution boundaries and tests. Reuse shared subprocess helpers if useful, but do not leave stale grooming errors labeled as scope refinement failures.
data.title: Fix stale grooming timeout ownership
